skill.name: scientific-writing
skill.description: Guides scientific manuscript drafting, revision, citation formatting, reporting guideline checks, figure and table planning, and professional LaTeX report formatting.
skill.summary: Draft and revise scientific manuscripts with structure, citations, and reporting standards.
skill.author: K-Dense-AI

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many high-risk patterns, but most are false positives from Markdown, LaTeX syntax, relative figure paths, and reference URLs. The remaining real risk is bounded: the skill allows Bash and gives local document-generation commands, so users should review commands before execution. No prompt injection or malicious exfiltration evidence was found.

medium_findings.title: Bash-enabled workflow with local command examples
medium_findings.description: The skill declares Bash access and includes examples that run Python figure generators and LaTeX compilers. This is legitimate for manuscript production, but it can execute local commands and write output files.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The command examples are explicit and Bash is allowed. Confidence is not higher because the commands are normal document-generation operations, not evidence of malicious intent.

low_findings.title: Markdown and LaTeX syntax misclassified as execution or weak crypto
low_findings.description: Many static hits are inline Markdown code, fenced LaTeX examples, or LaTeX package commands. These are documentation and typesetting syntax, not Ruby backtick execution or cryptographic code.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines show Markdown formatting, LaTeX environments, and package declarations. No interpreter invocation or cryptographic operation appears in those examples.

low_findings.title: Relative figure paths are LaTeX examples, not path traversal
low_findings.description: The path traversal alerts point to LaTeX includegraphics examples using parent-directory figure paths. They do not read arbitrary user paths or implement file access logic.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited paths are static LaTeX image references inside examples. There is no code that accepts user-controlled paths or traverses the filesystem.

low_findings.title: Reference URLs are documentation links, not network beacons
low_findings.description: Hardcoded URL alerts point to DOI examples, citation resources, and reporting guideline websites. The files do not contain fetch, curl, or other network-call logic.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URLs are visibly presented as human reference links and citation examples. No evidence found of automated network access or data exfiltration.

low_findings.title: Scientific review language misclassified as reconnaissance
low_findings.description: System reconnaissance alerts appear to be triggered by scientific terms such as review, evaluate, and check. The surrounding context is manuscript quality guidance, not host or network discovery.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed text concerns peer review, manuscript validity, and reporting checklists. No host enumeration or network scanning instructions were found.

dangerous_patterns.title: Bash-enabled workflow with local command examples
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill declares Bash access and includes examples that run Python figure generators and LaTeX compilers. This is legitimate for manuscript production, but it can execute local commands and write output files.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The command examples are explicit and Bash is allowed. Confidence is not higher because the commands are normal document-generation operations, not evidence of malicious intent.

content.value_statement: Scientific manuscripts require clear structure, accurate citations, and study-specific reporting standards. This skill guides drafting, revision, formatting, and submission preparation for research documents.
actual_capabilities: Structures manuscripts with IMRAD and related scientific formats.
Guides drafting of abstracts, methods, results, discussions, and cover letters.
Formats citations for APA, AMA, Vancouver, Chicago, and IEEE styles.
Maps study types to CONSORT, STROBE, PRISMA, and other reporting guidelines.
Provides guidance for figures, tables, graphical abstracts, and legends.
Includes LaTeX report templates and a professional scientific style package.
limitations: It does not verify citations against live bibliographic databases.
It does not judge scientific validity without user-provided evidence.
It may suggest local commands that users should review before running.
It relies on separate tools for literature lookup and image generation.

use_cases.title: Draft thesis manuscripts
use_cases.description: Turn study notes into structured manuscript sections with clear objectives, methods, results, and discussion.

use_cases.title: Prepare journal submissions
use_cases.description: Align manuscripts with citation styles, reporting checklists, figure requirements, and journal expectations.

use_cases.title: Create technical reports
use_cases.description: Produce polished scientific reports, white papers, and technical documents with consistent LaTeX formatting.

prompt_templates.title: Plan a Paper
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me outline a scientific paper about [topic]. Use IMRAD structure and list the evidence needed for each section.

prompt_templates.title: Draft a Section
prompt_templates.prompt: Write the [section name] for this study using complete paragraphs. Use these notes: [notes]. Keep claims tied to evidence.

prompt_templates.title: Apply Guidelines
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this [study type] manuscript for [CONSORT/STROBE/PRISMA] compliance. Identify missing checklist items and needed revisions.

prompt_templates.title: Prepare Submission
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced: adapt for a target journal.
prompt_templates.prompt: Adapt this manuscript for [journal name]. Check structure, citations, figures, required statements, and cover letter priorities.

output_examples.input: Draft an introduction for a clinical study on remote monitoring after heart failure discharge.
output_examples.output: The response would frame disease burden, identify the care-transition gap, summarize prior monitoring evidence, and state a focused study objective.

output_examples.input: Check a systematic review draft for PRISMA readiness.
output_examples.output: The response would identify missing eligibility criteria, search strategy details, screening flow information, bias assessment, and synthesis methods.

output_examples.input: Improve a results section with three tables and two figures.
output_examples.output: The response would order findings by research question, describe tables objectively, cite figures in sequence, and avoid unsupported interpretation.

best_practices: Provide study design, sample details, analysis methods, and target journal before drafting.
Use outlines for planning, then convert them into full paragraphs for final manuscript text.
Verify every citation, statistic, and reporting checklist item before submission.
anti_patterns: Do not ask for unsupported claims, invented citations, or overstated conclusions.
Do not mix citation styles or journal requirements within one manuscript.
Do not run suggested local commands without reviewing paths and inputs first.

faq.question: Which manuscript sections can this skill help write?
faq.answer: It can help with abstracts, introductions, methods, results, discussions, titles, figure legends, reports, and cover letters.

faq.question: Does it perform literature searches?
faq.answer: No. It expects supplied sources or a separate research lookup tool for evidence gathering.

faq.question: Can it format citations?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes guidance for APA, AMA, Vancouver, Chicago, IEEE, and common journal citation needs.

faq.question: Does it support reporting guidelines?
faq.answer: Yes. It covers CONSORT, STROBE, PRISMA, SPIRIT, STARD, TRIPOD, ARRIVE, CARE, CHEERS, and SRQR.

faq.question: Can it create figures?
faq.answer: It provides figure and table guidance, but actual image generation depends on separate tools.

faq.question: Why is the security risk medium?
faq.answer: The content is mostly documentation, but the skill allows Bash and includes local command examples.
